'''Martin Crompton''' (born 29 September 1969) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s, usually as a {{rlp|sh}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
===Warrington===
Born in [[Ince-in-Makerfield]], [[Wigan]], Crompton initially played for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]. Crompton played {{rlp|so}} in [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]'s 14–36 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1990 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1989–90 Rugby Football League season#Challenge Cup|1989–90 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 28 April 1990, in front of a crowd of 77,729.<ref name="1989-1990 Challenge Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=455|title=1989-1990 Challenge Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2016|accessdate=1 January 2017|archive-date=10 May 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170510181353/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=455|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Wigan===
Crompton moved to his hometown club [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in 1992, following the sale of Andy Gregory.<ref>{{cite news |title=Jackson matches up to Reilly criteria |work=The Guardian |date=10 July 1992 |location=London |page=18 |id={{ProQuest|187233458}}}}</ref> The transfer fee was decided via tribunal, with Wigan paying £65,000 to Warrington, plus an additional £20,000 if Crompton represented [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Wires upset by RL ruling |url=https://www.britishnewspaperarchive.co.uk/viewer/bl/0004985/19920801/050/0050 |work=Manchester Evening News |date=1 August 1992 |page=50 |via=[[British Newspaper Archive]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref> However, coach John Monie chose to play Shaun Edwards and Frano Botica at half back, resulting in Crompton's first team starts being seldom. During the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93 season]], Crompton appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing {{rlp|wg}} [[Martin Offiah]]) in [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]'s 5–4 victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[1992 Lancashire Cup]] Final at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St Helens]] on 18 October 1992.<ref name="1992-1993 Lancashire Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2745|title=1992–1993 Lancashire Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-date=13 July 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180713170348/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2745|url-status=dead}}</ref> Crompton also played from the interchange bench in the [[1992 World Club Challenge]] against the visiting [[Brisbane Broncos]] on 30 October 1992.

===Later career===
After a season with Wigan, Crompton went to [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in September 1993,<ref>{{cite news |title=Crompton signs for Oldham at long last |url=https://www.britishnewspaperarchive.co.uk/viewer/bl/0004985/19930907/047/0047 |work=Manchester Evening News |date=7 September 1993 |page=47 |via=[[British Newspaper Archive]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref> and became captain of the Roughyeds. When Oldham was relegated from [[Super League]], Crompton went to [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]]. After a few seasons with Salford, he moved to [[Widnes Vikings]], helping the club win promotion to the Super League. He retired from rugby league but was an assistant coach at [[Widnes]].

===International career===
Crompton was named in [[England national rugby league team|England]]'s 40-man training squad in preparation for the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]],<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Rugby League: Larder leaves out Schofield |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-larder-leaves-out-schofield-1592364.html |website=The Independent |access-date=23 October 2024 |date=19 July 1995}}</ref> but chose to represent [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] instead, and played for the team in the [[1995 Rugby League Emerging Nations Tournament|1995 Emerging Nations Tournament]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Dymock delays his decision |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/dymock-delays-his-decision-1601051.html |website=The Independent |access-date=23 October 2024 |date=13 September 1995}}</ref> He was also selected for Ireland at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]].

==Coaching career==
Crompton was appointed as head coach at [[Blackpool Panthers]] in 2007. In April 2008, he oversaw the club's first win in nearly two years against [[Workington Town]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Panthers end 45-game winless run |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7334764.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=23 October 2024 |date=7 April 2008}}</ref> The club improved over the next couple of seasons, reaching the playoffs in the [[2009 Championship 1|2009]] and [[2010 Championship 1|2010]] Championship 1 seasons. However, the club went into administration in October 2010, and Crompton resigned shortly afterwards.<ref>{{cite web |title=Blackpool Panthers enter crisis talks over future |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/9146981.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=23 October 2024 |date=1 November 2010}}</ref>
